name: Newcastle-Ottawa Quality Assessment Scale (Cohort Studies)
overall_judgment_rule: Sum stars across all items (max 9). AHRQ thresholds: Good = 3-4 selection AND 1-2 comparability AND 2-3 outcome stars; Fair = 2 selection AND 1-2 comparability AND 2-3 outcome; Poor = 0-1 selection OR 0 comparability OR 0-1 outcome.

domains.name: Selection: Representativeness of the exposed cohort
signaling_questions: How representative is the exposed cohort of the average target population (truly representative, somewhat representative, selected group, or no description)?
domains.criteria_summary: 1 star: truly representative of average target population (consecutive/random/community sample) OR somewhat representative. 0 stars: selected group OR no description.

domains.name: Selection: Selection of the non-exposed cohort
signaling_questions: Was the non-exposed cohort drawn from the same community as the exposed cohort?
domains.criteria_summary: 1 star: drawn from the same community as the exposed cohort. 0 stars: drawn from a different source OR no description.

domains.name: Selection: Ascertainment of exposure
signaling_questions: How was the exposure ascertained (secure record, structured interview, self-report, or no description)?
domains.criteria_summary: 1 star: secure record (e.g., surgical/medical record) OR structured interview. 0 stars: written self-report, no description, or other.

domains.name: Selection: Demonstration that outcome of interest was not present at start of study
retrieval_queries: prior history of outcome at enrollment
signaling_questions: Was it demonstrated that the outcome of interest was not present at the start of the study?
domains.criteria_summary: 1 star: demonstrated outcome not present at baseline (e.g., participants free of outcome at enrollment). 0 stars: no such demonstration.

domains.name: Comparability: Comparability of cohorts on the basis of design or analysis
signaling_questions: Did the study control for the most important confounder (e.g., age, sex)?
Did the study control for any additional important factors?
domains.criteria_summary: 1 star: controls for the most important confounder (e.g., age and sex). +1 additional star: controls for any other important factor (TBSA, comorbidities, etc.). 0 stars: cohorts not comparable.

domains.name: Outcome: Assessment of outcome
signaling_questions: How was the outcome assessed (independent blind assessment, record linkage, self-report, or no description)?
domains.criteria_summary: 1 star: independent blind assessment OR record linkage. 0 stars: self-report, no description, or other.

domains.name: Outcome: Was follow-up long enough for outcomes to occur
signaling_questions: Was follow-up long enough for outcomes to occur, given the typical latency of the outcome?
domains.criteria_summary: 1 star: follow-up sufficient for the outcome to plausibly occur (paper-specific judgment). 0 stars: too short.

domains.name: Outcome: Adequacy of follow-up of cohorts
retrieval_queries: loss to follow up complete attrition
subjects lost to follow up percentage
signaling_questions: Was follow-up of cohorts complete (all accounted for, lost <=20% with description, lost >20%, or no statement)?
domains.criteria_summary: 1 star: complete follow-up OR loss <=20% with description showing those lost are not different. 0 stars: loss >20% with no description, or no statement.
